title: Definitive JSA/JHA Form Structure for Industrial Construction
description: Research-backed JSA form structure synthesized from OSHA 3071, major EPC contractors (Bechtel, Fluor, Kiewit, Jacobs, Turner Industries), and refinery owner requirements (ExxonMobil OIM, Chevron OEMS, Shell HSSE, Valero PSM). Designed to be completed in 2-3 minutes with AI assistance.

research_sources.osha: OSHA Publication 3071 - Job Hazard Analysis (revised 2002), 29 CFR 1926 Construction Standards
research_sources.epc_contractors: Bechtel Zero Incident Program, Fluor HSE Management System, Kiewit Safety Program, Jacobs BeyondZero, Turner Industries Safety Manual
research_sources.refinery_owners: ExxonMobil Operations Integrity Management System (OIMS), Chevron Operational Excellence Management System (OEMS), Shell HSSE & SP Control Framework, Valero Process Safety Management
research_sources.industry_standards: ANSI/ASSP Z590.3-2011 Prevention Through Design, API RP 2220, NFPA 70E, CSA Z1002

form_metadata.form_name: Job Safety Analysis / Job Hazard Analysis
form_metadata.valid_duration: One shift only (must be renewed each shift)
form_metadata.retention: Minimum 1 year per OSHA recordkeeping; most owners require 3 years
review_triggers: Change in environmental conditions (weather, adjacent work)

sections.description: Who, what, where, when. Auto-populated where possible.

fields.notes: Auto-fills today's date. Calendar picker for backdating (supervisor only).
Auto-detect from current time. Day=0500-1700, Night=1700-0500.
Pulled from user's current project assignment. Dropdown of active projects.

fields.placeholder: e.g., Unit 12 Pipe Rack Level 3, MCC Room B, Tank Farm East
fields.notes: Free text with autocomplete from recently used locations.
From user's employer record.
From user's trade. Dropdown for manual override.

sections.description: Everyone who will perform or supervise this work.

fields.notes: The person filling out the JSA. Auto-populated from login.
Auto-populated from org chart. Dropdown of available supervisors.
Multi-select from roster. Each crew member must acknowledge the JSA. Minimum 1 crew member (the preparer). Bechtel/Fluor require all names; refineries require badge numbers too.
Auto-calculated from crew list.

sections.description: Clear description of the work to be performed.

fields.placeholder: e.g., Install 4-inch conduit run from MCC-12 to JB-4A on pipe rack elevation 35 ft. Pull 3/C #6 AWG wire through existing conduit.
fields.notes: Voice input primary. Should be specific enough that a safety officer reading it knows exactly what you're doing. OSHA 3071 says: describe the job, not just the task name.
Multiple can apply (e.g., Turnaround + Maintenance).

sections.description: What permits are needed and what conditions affect the work.

fields.notes: Multi-select checkboxes. This is a critical field — missing permits is the #1 reason JSAs get rejected by safety.
Optional at draft stage. Required before work begins. Refineries (especially ExxonMobil) require this linked.
This drives automatic PPE and hazard suggestions. Weather conditions auto-suggested from weather API.

options: Low - Routine task, familiar crew, standard conditions
Medium - Some elevated hazards, non-routine elements
High - Multiple serious hazards, critical lift, energized work, confined space
Critical - Life-threatening potential, requires site management approval
fields.notes: Drives approval requirements. Critical risk requires superintendent + site safety manager sign-off. Chevron and Shell require risk matrix scoring.

sections.description: The core of the JSA. Break the job into sequential steps, identify hazards for each, and define controls. OSHA 3071 methodology: (1) Sequence the job steps, (2) Identify the hazards, (3) Determine preventive measures.

columns.notes: Keep steps specific and action-oriented. Start with a verb. E.g., 'Set up ladder at column C-12' not 'Preparation'. OSHA recommends breaking the job into 5-10 steps.
Identify ALL hazards for this step. AI suggests from hazard library based on keywords. Common categories: Struck-by, Caught-in, Fall, Electrical, Burns, Chemical, Ergonomic, Environmental.
Some owners (Chevron, Shell) use a 5x5 risk matrix (Severity x Likelihood). For mobile, a simple L/M/H/C is faster and sufficient for most contractors.

Must follow Hierarchy of Controls (OSHA preferred order): 1) Elimination, 2) Substitution, 3) Engineering Controls, 4) Administrative Controls, 5) PPE (last resort). AI suggests controls from knowledge base. Must be specific — 'Be careful' is NOT a control. Good: 'Use fall arrest harness tied off to beam clamp at column C-12, 100% tie-off during transition.'
hierarchy_of_controls: Substitution - Replace with less hazardous alternative
Engineering Controls - Guardrails, ventilation, barriers, GFCI
Administrative Controls - Training, procedures, permits, signage, buddy system
PPE - Last line of defense, specify exact PPE type

columns.notes: Who is responsible for ensuring this control is in place. Usually initials. Some owners skip this column.

table_structure.notes: OSHA recommends 5-10 steps. Less than 3 means you haven't broken the job down enough. More than 10 means you should split into multiple JSAs. AI can auto-generate initial steps from the task description.

sections.description: All personal protective equipment required for this job. Standard site PPE is pre-checked. Additional PPE based on hazards identified above.

items: Hard Hat (ANSI Z89.1 Type I, Class E)
Steel/Composite Toe Boots (ASTM F2413 EH rated)
Long Pants (no shorts on industrial sites)
fields.notes: These are ALWAYS required on industrial construction sites. Pre-checked and locked. Cannot be unchecked. This is universal across Bechtel, Fluor, Kiewit, all refineries.

Head Protection: Type II Hard Hat (side impact) - for overhead/confined work
Balaclava/Hard Hat Liner - for cold weather
Face Shield (ANSI Z87.1) - for grinding, chipping, chemical
Eye/Face Protection: Tinted Safety Glasses - for outdoor glare
Face Shield with Hard Hat Bracket
Hand Protection: Rubber Insulating Gloves with Leather Protectors (Class 00-4)
Foot Protection: Metatarsal Guards - for heavy material handling
Body Protection: FR Clothing (CAT 3 / 25 cal/cm2) - for higher arc flash
FR Clothing (CAT 4 / 40 cal/cm2) - for high-energy arc flash
Rain Gear (FR-rated if in FR area)

fields.notes: AI auto-suggests based on hazards in Section 5. Worker confirms/adds. Each PPE item links to the hazard it mitigates.

sections.description: What to do if something goes wrong. Auto-populated from site data.

fields.notes: Auto-populated from project/site settings.
Auto-suggested based on work area. Dropdown of site muster points.
Auto-populated from site data based on work area.
Required when chemical hazards are present. Auto-populated.

fields.placeholder: How will an injured worker be rescued from height or confined space?
fields.notes: REQUIRED when fall protection or confined space is involved. OSHA 1926.502(d)(20) requires rescue plan for fall protection. This is a frequent audit finding.

sections.description: Every crew member must review and acknowledge the JSA before work begins. This is the toolbox talk / pre-task briefing.

fields.notes: Confirms the crew discussed this JSA together before starting work. OSHA and all major contractors require this.

fields.field_label: Stop Work Authority Reviewed with Crew
fields.notes: Every contractor (Bechtel, Fluor, Kiewit) and every refinery (ExxonMobil, Chevron, Shell, Valero) requires this. Non-negotiable.

Each crew member signs or provides electronic acknowledgment. For mobile: tap to acknowledge with timestamp. Bechtel requires wet signatures on paper; digital accepted for Fluor, Kiewit, Turner Industries. Refineries vary — ExxonMobil accepts digital in newer facilities.

sections.description: Chain of approval. Order matters: Worker signs first, then Foreman, then Safety (if required by risk level).

approval_chain.label: Prepared By (Lead Worker / Journeyman)
approval_chain.notes: The person who filled out the JSA. Always required.

approval_chain.label: Foreman / Supervisor Approval
approval_chain.notes: Must review the JSA for completeness, verify hazards are properly identified, controls are adequate. Cannot approve their own JSA (conflict of interest). Required by ALL contractors and owners.

approval_chain.label: Safety Representative Approval
approval_chain.condition: risk_level == High OR risk_level == Critical OR permits include Confined Space OR permits include LOTO OR permits include Energized Work
approval_chain.notes: Required for High/Critical risk work. ExxonMobil requires safety approval on ALL JSAs. Chevron requires it for any permit-required work. Bechtel requires for high-risk only. Some sites require safety to approve every JSA — this is configurable.

approval_chain.label: Superintendent / Area Manager Approval
approval_chain.notes: Only for Critical risk tasks. Shell and ExxonMobil require superintendent sign-off for critical lifts, energized work >480V, confined space with IDLH atmosphere.

rejection_criteria.title: Common Reasons JSAs Get Rejected
rejection_criteria.description: Based on safety department audit data from major contractors and refineries.
automatic_rejection: No hazards identified (at least 1 hazard per step required)
Generic controls like 'Be careful' or 'Use caution' or 'Pay attention' — must be specific
PPE is the ONLY control listed (must use hierarchy of controls — engineering/admin first)
Missing required permits (hot work near flammables, confined space entry, etc.)
Crew members not listed or not acknowledged
Work at height identified but no fall protection plan
Confined space identified but no rescue plan
LOTO required but not listed in permits
Task description is too vague (e.g., 'electrical work' instead of specific scope)
Risk level doesn't match hazards (high hazards but marked Low risk)
common_audit_findings: JSA was written at the desk, not at the work site — hazards don't match actual conditions
Copy-paste from yesterday's JSA without reviewing for today's conditions
Crew members signed but clearly didn't participate in the discussion
Hazard analysis doesn't match the actual sequence of work steps
Environmental conditions not updated (weather changed, adjacent work started)
JSA written after work already started (must be completed BEFORE work begins)
No evidence of crew discussion / toolbox talk
Fall protection controls don't specify tie-off points
Electrical hazards listed but no arc flash boundary or voltage specified
Chemical hazards listed but no SDS referenced or available

ai_assistance_features.description: How AI helps complete the JSA in 2-3 minutes

ai_generated_suggestions.action: AI parses task description and suggests: (1) job steps broken into sequence, (2) likely hazards per step from hazard library, (3) recommended controls from knowledge base, (4) required PPE based on hazards, (5) required permits based on task type, (6) risk level assessment
AI suggests: (1) environmental conditions for that area, (2) adjacent hazards, (3) emergency info for that zone
AI pre-loads trade-specific hazard library and standard PPE for that trade
AI adds relevant hazards and controls (e.g., Heat selected -> adds hydration breaks, buddy system, shade requirements)

voice_workflow.description: Journeyman speaks naturally, AI structures into JSA
voice_workflow.example_voice_input: We're pulling wire on the pipe rack at unit 12, about 35 feet up. Four guys on the crew — me, Tommy, Carlos, and Mike. We'll be setting up the tugger at grade, running the pull rope up through the conduit, then pulling the wire. Main concerns are working at height, heavy cable weight, and there's hot work going on one bay over.
voice_workflow.ai_output: AI extracts: Location (Unit 12 pipe rack, 35 ft), Crew (4 members), Steps (1. Set up tugger, 2. Run pull rope, 3. Pull wire), Hazards (fall from height, struck by cable, ergonomic - heavy lifting, adjacent hot work), Controls (fall harness + SRL, tag line on cable, mechanical advantage/tugger, fire watch awareness), PPE (standard + harness + SRL + face shield for adjacent hot work), Permits (elevated work, hot work awareness)

design_principles: Voice-first: speak the task description, AI fills in the rest
One-tap selections: big touch targets, dropdowns not typing
Pre-populated: 60% of fields auto-fill from user profile and site data
Progressive disclosure: show required fields first, expand for details
Offline capable: must work without internet on job site
2-3 minute target: from open to submitted in under 3 minutes
